replies.author: @DerDudelino
replies.text:
I always liked well written game journalism - GameInformer cover stories, what EDGE does and so on. But sooo many US websites have basically been invaded by culture warriors that don't give a flying fuck about games. 

Every time I've read a Polygon review, I've questioned whether they've actually played the game because they would rage about some nonsense for 80 percent of the article and had only like 3 sentences in it about gameplay, storytelling, character development, world building -you know the stuff people care about :D

author: @j95lee
text:
In the 90s I was a subscriber to the Gamepro and EGM. For the kids - Subscriptions back then meant you got a monthly issue through the mail. I looked forward to new issues every month.

There was hardly any politics in those magazines, other than some jabs at Clinton. Articles covering government scrutiny on violent games like mortal kombat and night trap never devolved into  hyper partisanship. Console fan boy wars, yeah that was still a thing.

Those mags were also fun to read.   They had personality. Mad magazine, highlights, Disney adventures - all fun to read. Now humor sites are clickbait traps and game journalism is impersonal recap at best and won’t at worst.

author: @MavisRecon
text:
Polygon is now owned by Valnet, which is owned by Valsef. Look at the guy who owns that.

Ever notice how all of these companies we successfully put out of business for going woke never (and I mean NEVER) end up getting sold to an American or American entity? 

I realized a long time ago (when I saw companies at large continue on being woke even after watching wokeness destroy everything it ever touches) that there has to be something else going on here. The woke strategy obviously doesn't work, but they kept doing it anyway. That doesn't sound like true multi-million and multi-billion dollar American marketing strategies, ever. In history. They've never behaved like this before.

As a result, I hate to play devil's advocate, but it seems like these companies are being extorted, forced into woke-ism via whatever avenue (blackmail of some sort?), stocks tank, then a foreign entity buys them--rinse and repeat. And I bet you'll see this a lot more with China in the near future too. Tencent and Ubisoft was just the beginning.
